title: Id Give You A Cookie But I Ate It
section_path: Id Give You A Cookie But I Ate It
section_path_text: Id Give You A Cookie But I Ate It
summary:
Main
Earlier this month, Beijing introduced a series of economic measures which comprised simultaneously for the first time, rate cuts, a reduction in the RRR (Reserve Requirement Ratio) and structural monetary policies. While still facing in-depth structural economic changes, the stimulus objectives are to revamp the property sector which grew to become a main driver of Chinese economic growth over the past decade. Whereas those measures are meant to boost consumer confidence, the real estate crisis combined with deflationary pressures appear to be a millstone to China's economic rebound.
